text: A tool is a scripted tool a player holds in their hand. The tool object is focused on handling animations for first and third person view.
For more information about creating your own scripted tools, see [ToolClass, ToolClass].
The interact state describe what kind of interaction is made. This is used to check whether a mouse button or key was just made pressed, held, etc.
Used to check collisions between the lift and the world.
params: A table of all the bodies belonging to the creation placed on the lift.
The rotation of the creation on the lift.
return: boolean, integer					True if the lift collides with the world; The lift level.

text: Checks whether a specific lift level is valid (no collision) for a creation at the given position and rotation.
params: A table of all the bodies belonging to the creation placed on the lift.
The rotation of the creation on the lift.
The specific lift level to test.
The timestamp of when the body set was initialized
return: boolean								True if the level is valid (no collision).

text: Find index of column of shades that best matches given color.
Force equip a tool for the local player. Pass nil to unforce an already forced tool.
Returns the maximum number of lift levels.
return: integer								The maximum lift level count.

text: Pre-loads renderable data to be used by the tool. This eliminates excessive loading during run time.
Return whether the tool uuid exists
